topic_title: Off-site data-centre labour logistics comparators gigafactories and 10 jobs per MW rule
research_goal: Reproducible re-run of the 2026-06-29 supplemental deep search (formerly manual ChatGPT + Gemini). Targeted evidence search — not a general narrative review. Baseline context: direct data-centre construction intensity about 0.7-2.0 peak workers/MW and operational intensity about 0.15-0.35 FTE/MW. Research questions: (1) What source-backed evidence exists on off-site prefabrication, modular MEP, equipment manufacturing, or supply-chain labour for data-centre construction? (2) Are there citable metrics for logistics/warehousing/fulfilment-centre construction and operations jobs — jobs per floor area, jobs per capex, peak construction workforce, construction job-years, operational jobs per facility? (3) Are there citable metrics for advanced manufacturing/gigafactory construction and operations in comparable units? (4) Is there any credible source for a '10 jobs/MW during construction versus 1 job/MW during operations' rule for data centres — if yes, identify source, exact unit, scope, geography, and whether peak jobs, annual FTE, job-years, direct, or total-supported. Prioritise peer-reviewed papers, government/parliamentary reports, planning approvals and development agreements, transparent industry reports, then company announcements with clear capex/scale/jobs. Do not merge on-site construction with off-site prefabrication unless the source does. Do not treat enabled AI/IT jobs as facility-attributable. Do not use 10 jobs/MW vs 1 job/MW without clear source and unit. Deliver: evidence memo, usable metric table, rejected-source table, recommendation on expanding beyond energy comparators. Minimum yield bar: two citable sources with compatible units per new comparator class.
role_descriptor: expert systematic reviewer and construction-economics evidence curator
